findings.summary: Seat mutation proceeds with no identity verification — update_seat writes reservation state from a customer-typed confirmation number, and the system has no authentication or confirmation-number check at all.

The seat-booking handoff fabricates an authoritative flight number at runtime (random.randint) and update_seat asserts on it, treating agent-generated data as authoritative for a reservation mutation.
update_seat writes any model-supplied seat string with no check that the seat exists, is available, or is not already assigned to another passenger.

No durable audit log is written for seat changes — update_seat only returns a string and the only observability is ephemeral SDK tracing, leaving the consequential mutation with no accountable record.
No input, output, or tool guardrails are wired despite the SDK providing them, so prompt injection in customer input or tool output reaches the agents and their mutating tools unmitigated.
The seat-booking agent accepts the triage handoff and mutates reservation state without re-verifying identity, so trust and effective privilege cross the agent boundary unchecked.

Domain scope is enforced only in prompt text — the triage agent has no airline-topic restriction and the FAQ agent's 'do not rely on your own knowledge' rule has no code gate, so off-domain answers and speculation are possible.
